text:
Once upon a quiet town lived a curious chipmunk named Chester. Chester loved puzzles and mysteries. Every day after school he’d sneak quietly into the old library.

That particular evening, Chester discovered a dusty old book entitled "The Enchanted Garden". Intrigued, he flipped open the pages. There were drawings that felt alive. An old woman owl fluttered about, guiding a squirrel.

Curiosity got the better of him. Chester decided to visit her. She welcomed him warmly. Over tea, the owl spoke of secrets long forgotten. The garden had enchantments. To protect his secret home, Chester promised to keep them safe.

Each night, Chester quietly tended their needs—planting flowers, watering seeds. He learned tales old.

Years passed. The old lady passed away. Chester discovered her legacy in an alphabet book of owl stories. He wrote his own.

Now, Chester spends winters wandering forests weaving tales. His garden thrives. That's his treasure. And so, little
